paragraphs.en: Dear friends, the blue brick ground we're standing on now is the sacred place where emperors of the Ming and Qing dynasties "communicated with heaven"—the Temple of Heaven in Beijing! I'm your tour guide, Miya, and it's my great honor to be your companion today. Over the next 15 minutes, we'll enter from the South Gate and follow the route emperors took for heaven-worship: first visit the Circular Mound Altar (the core of heaven-worship), then walk along Danbi Bridge to the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests (the key site for harvest-prayer). Let's unlock the "heaven is round, earth is square" code of the Temple of Heaven and learn how our ancestors used architecture to connect with nature. Let's go!
paragraphs.zh: 各位朋友，咱们现在脚踩的这片青砖地，可是明清两代皇帝专门跟"上天对话"的圣地——北京天坛！接下来15分钟，咱们从南门进，顺着皇帝当年祭天的路线走：先逛"祭天核心"圜丘坛，再沿丹陛桥到"祈谷重地"祈年殿，一起解锁天坛的"天圆地方"密码，听听老祖宗怎么用建筑跟自然对话。咱们这就出发！

sections.heading_en: I. Historical Evolution: A "Heaven-Worship Project" by Three Emperors

paragraphs.en: Before visiting the scenic spots, let's talk about the "past and present" of the Temple of Heaven—its story is closely tied to three Ming emperors: Emperor Yongle (1420): He first built this altar, originally named "Altar of Heaven and Earth," where emperors worshiped both heaven and earth—it was the "prototype" of the Temple of Heaven; Emperor Jiajing (1530): He thought "worshiping heaven and earth separately is more respectful," so he moved the earth-worship part to the Temple of Earth. Since then, this place has been dedicated to heaven-worship, officially renamed "Temple of Heaven," and the current layout of "Circular Mound Altar in the south, Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests in the north" was established; Emperor Qianlong (Qianlong reign of the Qing Dynasty): He spent great efforts on renovation, such as replacing the Circular Mound Altar with better gray-white stone and reinforcing the wooden structure of the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests. The Temple of Heaven we see now is basically the appearance from the Qianlong period.
paragraphs.zh: 在逛景点前，先跟大家唠唠天坛的"前世今生"——它的故事离不开三位明朝皇帝：永乐皇帝（明永乐十八年，1420年）：最早建了这座坛，当时叫"天地坛"，皇帝既祭天又祭地，是天坛的"雏形"；嘉靖皇帝（明嘉靖九年，1530年）：觉得"天地分开祭才够恭敬"，就把祭地的部分搬到了地坛，这儿专门祭天，正式改名"天坛"，还定下了现在"南圜丘、北祈谷"的布局；乾隆皇帝（清乾隆年间）：又花了大力气修缮，比如给圜丘坛换了更好的青白石，给祈年殿加固了木构，咱们现在看到的天坛，基本是乾隆时期的模样。

sections.heading_en: II. Location & Layout: The Architectural Code of "Heaven Is Round, Earth Is Square"

paragraphs.en: First, let's clarify the location: the Temple of Heaven is in the southern part of Beijing, covering an area of about 2.73 million square meters, 3.8 times the size of the Forbidden City! Its layout is entirely centered on the ancient Chinese view of the universe—"heaven is round, earth is square": The Circular Mound Altar area in the south is square, corresponding to "square earth," specially used for heaven-worship; The Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests area in the north is circular, corresponding to "round heaven," specially used for harvest-prayer; A 360-meter-long "Danbi Bridge" connects the two areas like a "heavenly road," linking the heaven-worship and harvest-prayer functional zones. When emperors walked on it, it was like moving from "the human world" to "the heavenly realm."
paragraphs.zh: 咱们先明确个位置：天坛在北京市南部，占地约273万平方米，相当于3.8个故宫那么大！它的布局全围绕"天圆地方"这一古人的宇宙观：南边的圜丘坛区域是方形的，对应"地方"，专门用来祭天；北边的祈谷坛区域是圆形的，对应"天圆"，专门用来祈谷；中间用一条长360米的"丹陛桥"连接，像一条"天路"，把祭天和祈谷两个功能区串起来，皇帝走在上面，就像从"人间"走向"天界"。

sections.heading_en: III. Circular Mound Altar – The Emperor's "Heaven-Worship Stage" — 1. Architectural Complex & Function

paragraphs.en: We're now standing at the Circular Mound Altar, the "core of heaven-worship" in the Temple of Heaven. Every Winter Solstice, emperors worshiped heaven here, and on the first Xin day of the first lunar month, they prayed for rain here. The complex of the Circular Mound Altar is simple, with only three "treasures": Circular Mound (core stone platform): A three-tiered circular platform made of gray-white stone, the "main stage" for emperors to worship heaven; Imperial Vault of Heaven (small hall in the north): Before heaven-worship, the divine tablet of "Heavenly Emperor" was invited from here to the Circular Mound, and sent back after the ceremony—it was equivalent to a "temporary bedroom" for heaven; Echo Wall (wall around the Imperial Vault of Heaven): A circular wall surrounding the Imperial Vault of Heaven, serving as an "auxiliary space" for the heaven-worship ceremony and also a popular check-in spot of the Temple of Heaven.
paragraphs.zh: 咱们现在站的就是圜丘坛，它是天坛的"祭天核心"，每年冬至皇帝在这儿祭天，正月上辛日在这儿祈雨。圜丘坛的组群很简单，就三件"宝贝"：圜丘（核心石台）：三层圆形青白石台，是皇帝祭天的"主舞台"；皇穹宇（北边小殿）：祭天前，把"皇天上帝"的神牌从这儿请去圜丘，祭完再送回来，相当于上天的"临时寝宫"；回音壁（皇穹宇围墙）：环绕皇穹宇的圆形墙，是祭天仪式的"辅助空间"，也成了天坛的"网红打卡点"。

paragraphs.en: Take a close look at the details of the Circular Mound—they are all "nine" and "multiples of nine"—because ancient people believed "nine" was the largest yang number, corresponding to "heaven": The diameter of the upper platform is 9 zhang (1 zhang ≈ 3.33 meters), the middle platform is 15 zhang (15=9+6, a yang number), and the lower platform is 21 zhang (21=9×2+3, a yang number); The number of steps and balustrades on each tier is a multiple of nine: for example, the upper platform has 9 circles of stone slabs, with 9 slabs in the innermost circle, 9 more slabs in each outer circle, and exactly 81 slabs (9×9) in the 9th circle. These numbers are not coincidences—they are all to show respect for heaven!
paragraphs.zh: 大家仔细看圜丘的细节，全是"9"和"9的倍数"——这是因为古人认为"9"是最大的阳数，对应"天"：上层台面直径9丈，中层15丈（15=9+6，属阳数），下层21丈（21=9×2+3，属阳数）；每一层的台阶数、栏板数都是9的倍数：比如上层有9圈石板，最内圈9块，往外每圈多9块，第9圈正好81块（9×9）。这些数字不是巧合，全是为了体现对上天的敬畏！

paragraphs.en: There are two "acoustic miracles" at the Circular Mound Altar: Heaven's Heart Stone: The stone slab in the center of the Circular Mound is called "Heaven's Heart Stone." Standing on it and speaking softly, your voice will be particularly loud, like an echo—this is the ancients using the principle of sound reflection and superposition to make the "Son of Heaven's" voice "reach heaven"; Echo Wall: If two people stand at the east and west ends of the Echo Wall respectively and speak softly to the wall, the other person can hear clearly, like a "natural walkie-talkie"—because the wall is smooth and hard, sound can reflect continuously along the wall without dissipation.
paragraphs.zh: 圜丘坛有两个"声学奇迹"：天心石：圜丘中心的石板叫"天心石"，站在上面轻声说话，声音会特别洪亮，像有回声——这是古人利用声音反射叠加的原理，让"天子"的声音能"传到上天"；回音壁：两个人分别站在回音壁的东西两端，对着墙小声说话，对方能清楚听到，像"天然对讲机"——因为墙面光滑坚硬，声音能沿墙连续反射，不散失。

sections.heading_en: 4. Nine Rituals of the Heaven-Worship Ceremony

paragraphs.en: In the past, the emperor's heaven-worship ceremony was very grand, with nine steps: 1) Welcoming the Heavenly Emperor: Inviting the divine tablet of "Heavenly Emperor" to the altar; 2) Offering Jade and Silk: Presenting jade bi and silk to show respect; 3) Offering Sacrifices: Presenting animal sacrifices; 4) First Toast: The emperor toasts for the first time and reads the memorial to heaven; 5) Second Toast: The emperor toasts for the second time; 6) Third Toast: The emperor toasts for the third time; 7) Removing Sacrifices: Taking away the sacrifices; 8) Seeing Off the Heavenly Emperor: Sending the divine tablet back to the Imperial Vault of Heaven; 9) Watching the Burning: Burning the memorial and sacrifices to let "heaven" receive them. Every step was accompanied by ritual music, creating a very solemn scene.
paragraphs.zh: 当年皇帝祭天，仪式特别隆重，分九步走：1)迎帝神：把"皇天上帝"的神牌请上祭台；2)奠玉帛：献上玉璧和丝绸；3)进俎：献上牲畜祭品；4)初献：皇帝第一次敬酒，读祭文；5)亚献：皇帝第二次敬酒；6)终献：皇帝第三次敬酒；7)撤馔：撤走祭品；8)送帝神：把神牌送回皇穹宇；9)望燎：把祭文、祭品烧掉，让"上天"收到。

paragraphs.en: After visiting the Circular Mound Altar, let's walk north along the stone road—this is Danbi Bridge, also known as "Haimen Avenue." It looks flat, but in fact, it's high in the middle and low on both sides: the central imperial road is dedicated to the emperor, the left road is for princes and nobles, and the right road is for civil and military officials—hierarchy is clearly divided. Its role is "transition": from the "heavenly realm" of heaven-worship in the south to the "human world" of harvest-prayer in the north, embodying "the connection between heaven and man."
paragraphs.zh: 逛完圜丘坛，咱们沿着眼前这条石板路往北走——这就是丹陛桥，也叫"海墁大道"。它看着平，其实中间高、两边低：中间的御道专供皇帝走，左边是王公贵族的路，右边是文武百官的路，等级分得特别清。它的作用就是"过渡"：从南边祭天的"天界"，走到北边祈谷的"人间"，体现"天人相通"。

sections.heading_en: V. Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests – The Emperor's "Sacred Place for Harvest-Prayer" — 1. Architectural Complex & Function

paragraphs.en: After walking through Danbi Bridge, we arrive at the Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests, where emperors prayed for "abundant grain harvests" on the first Xin day of the first lunar month every year. The complex has three core buildings: Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests (main hall): Where emperors held the harvest-prayer ceremony, it's the iconic building of the Temple of Heaven; Gate of Prayer for Good Harvests (main gate): The gate to enter the Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests, with the plaque written by Emperor Qianlong himself; Hall of Imperial Heaven (small hall in the north): A place to store divine tablets for harvest-prayer, housing the divine tablets of "Heavenly Emperor" and ancestors.
paragraphs.zh: 走过丹陛桥，咱们就到了祈谷坛，这儿是皇帝每年正月上辛日"祈求五谷丰登"的地方，核心就是咱们眼前这座蓝色圆顶建筑——祈年殿。祈谷坛的组群有三座核心建筑：祈年殿（主殿）：皇帝举行祈谷大典的地方，是天坛的标志性建筑；祈年门（正门）：进入祈谷坛的大门，乾隆皇帝亲笔题的"祈年门"匾额还挂在上面；皇乾殿（北边小殿）：存放祈谷神牌的地方，里面供奉"皇天上帝"和列祖列宗的神牌。

sections.heading_en: 2. Architectural Features: A Wooden Structure Miracle

paragraphs.en: Take a close look at the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests—its "magic" is hidden in the details: Nail-free wooden structure: This 38-meter-high three-tiered circular hall is built entirely with wooden mortise and tenon structure without a single nail; Blue glazed tiles: The roof is covered with blue glazed tiles, because "blue corresponds to the sky," symbolizing connection with heaven; 28 nanmu pillars: The 28 nanmu pillars in the hall are divided into three tiers: the innermost 4 "Longjing Pillars" represent the four seasons, the middle 12 "Golden Pillars" represent the 12 months, and the outermost 12 "Eaves Pillars" represent the 12 two-hour periods of a day. The 28 pillars in total correspond to the 28 constellations—the ancients integrated "heaven, time, and seasons" into the building!
paragraphs.zh: 大家仔细看祈年殿，它的"神奇"藏在细节里：无钉木构：高38米的三层圆形大殿，全靠木榫卯结构搭建，没用一根钉子；蓝色琉璃瓦：屋顶覆盖蓝色琉璃瓦，因为"蓝色对应天空"，象征与天相通；28根楠木柱：殿内28根楠木柱分三层：最内层4根"龙井柱"代表四季，中间12根"金柱"代表12个月，最外层12根"檐柱"代表12时辰，28根合起来对应28星宿——古人把"天、时、节"全融进建筑里了！

paragraphs.en: In 1998, the Temple of Heaven was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, praised as "the pinnacle of ancient Chinese heaven-worship architecture, embodying the concept of harmonious coexistence between man and nature."
paragraphs.zh: 1998年，天坛被联合国教科文组织列入《世界遗产名录》，评价它是"中国古代祭天建筑的巅峰之作，体现了人与自然和谐共生的理念"。

paragraphs.en: Dear friends, our tour of the Temple of Heaven ends here! We walked from the "nine-number code" of the Circular Mound Altar to the "nail-free wooden structure" of the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests, listened to the "natural walkie-talkie" of the Echo Wall, and understood the "heaven-man road" of Danbi Bridge. This 600-year-old sacred place is not just architecture, but the original intention of the ancients to "respect heaven, love people, and pray for harvests." I hope today's explanation can help you remember the blue tiles and domes of the Temple of Heaven, as well as the respect for nature. Thank you for your company. Wish you a smooth journey. Goodbye!
paragraphs.zh: 各位朋友，天坛之旅到这儿就结束啦！咱们从圜丘坛的"9数密码"，走到祈年殿的"无钉木构"，听了回音壁的"天然对讲机"，也懂了丹陛桥的"天人之路"。这座600年的圣地，不只是建筑，更是古人"敬天、爱人、祈丰"的初心。希望今天的讲解，能让大家记住天坛的蓝瓦、圆顶，还有那份对自然的敬畏。感谢大家的陪伴，祝大家旅途顺利，再见！

sentences.zh: 在逛景点前，先跟大家唠唠天坛的"前世今生"——它的故事离不开三位明朝皇帝：永乐皇帝（明永乐十八年，1420年）：最早建了这座坛，当时叫"天地坛"，皇帝既祭天又祭地，是天坛的"雏形"；嘉靖皇帝（明嘉靖九年，1530年）：觉得"天地分开祭才够恭敬"，就把祭地的部分搬到了地坛，这儿专门祭天，正式改名"天坛"，还定下了现在"南圜丘、北祈谷"的布局；乾隆皇帝（清乾隆年间）：又花了大力气修缮，比如给圜丘坛换了更好的青白石，给祈年殿加固了木构，咱们现在看到的天坛，基本是乾隆时期的模样。

sentences.en: First, let's clarify the location: the Temple of Heaven is in the southern part of Beijing, covering an area of about 2.73 million square meters, 3.8 times the size of the Forbidden City!
sentences.zh: 咱们先明确个位置：天坛在北京市南部，占地约273万平方米，相当于3.8个故宫那么大！它的布局全围绕"天圆地方"这一古人的宇宙观：南边的圜丘坛区域是方形的，对应"地方"，专门用来祭天；北边的祈谷坛区域是圆形的，对应"天圆"，专门用来祈谷；中间用一条长360米的"丹陛桥"连接，像一条"天路"，把祭天和祈谷两个功能区串起来，皇帝走在上面，就像从"人间"走向"天界"。

sentences.en: We're now standing at the Circular Mound Altar, the "core of heaven-worship" in the Temple of Heaven.
sentences.zh: 咱们现在站的就是圜丘坛，它是天坛的"祭天核心"，每年冬至皇帝在这儿祭天，正月上辛日在这儿祈雨。圜丘坛的组群很简单，就三件"宝贝"：圜丘（核心石台）：三层圆形青白石台，是皇帝祭天的"主舞台"；皇穹宇（北边小殿）：祭天前，把"皇天上帝"的神牌从这儿请去圜丘，祭完再送回来，相当于上天的"临时寝宫"；回音壁（皇穹宇围墙）：环绕皇穹宇的圆形墙，是祭天仪式的"辅助空间"，也成了天坛的"网红打卡点"。
sentences.heading_en: III. Circular Mound Altar – The Emperor's "Heaven-Worship Stage" — 1. Architectural Complex & Function

sentences.en: Take a close look at the details of the Circular Mound—they are all "nine" and "multiples of nine"—because ancient people believed "nine" was the largest yang number, corresponding to "heaven":
sentences.zh: 大家仔细看圜丘的细节，全是"9"和"9的倍数"——这是因为古人认为"9"是最大的阳数，对应"天"：上层台面直径9丈，中层15丈（15=9+6，属阳数），下层21丈（21=9×2+3，属阳数）；每一层的台阶数、栏板数都是9的倍数：比如上层有9圈石板，最内圈9块，往外每圈多9块，第9圈正好81块（9×9）。这些数字不是巧合，全是为了体现对上天的敬畏！
sentences.heading_en: 2. Architectural Features: The Code of "Nine"

sentences.en: The diameter of the upper platform is 9 zhang (1 zhang ≈ 3.33 meters), the middle platform is 15 zhang (15=9+6, a yang number), and the lower platform is 21 zhang (21=9×2+3, a yang number);
sentences.zh: 大家仔细看圜丘的细节，全是"9"和"9的倍数"——这是因为古人认为"9"是最大的阳数，对应"天"：上层台面直径9丈，中层15丈（15=9+6，属阳数），下层21丈（21=9×2+3，属阳数）；每一层的台阶数、栏板数都是9的倍数：比如上层有9圈石板，最内圈9块，往外每圈多9块，第9圈正好81块（9×9）。这些数字不是巧合，全是为了体现对上天的敬畏！
sentences.heading_en: 2. Architectural Features: The Code of "Nine"

sentences.heading_en: 2. Architectural Features: The Code of "Nine"

sentences.en: These numbers are not coincidences—they are all to show respect for heaven!
sentences.zh: 大家仔细看圜丘的细节，全是"9"和"9的倍数"——这是因为古人认为"9"是最大的阳数，对应"天"：上层台面直径9丈，中层15丈（15=9+6，属阳数），下层21丈（21=9×2+3，属阳数）；每一层的台阶数、栏板数都是9的倍数：比如上层有9圈石板，最内圈9块，往外每圈多9块，第9圈正好81块（9×9）。这些数字不是巧合，全是为了体现对上天的敬畏！
sentences.heading_en: 2. Architectural Features: The Code of "Nine"

sentences.en: There are two "acoustic miracles" at the Circular Mound Altar:
sentences.zh: 圜丘坛有两个"声学奇迹"：天心石：圜丘中心的石板叫"天心石"，站在上面轻声说话，声音会特别洪亮，像有回声——这是古人利用声音反射叠加的原理，让"天子"的声音能"传到上天"；回音壁：两个人分别站在回音壁的东西两端，对着墙小声说话，对方能清楚听到，像"天然对讲机"——因为墙面光滑坚硬，声音能沿墙连续反射，不散失。
sentences.heading_en: 3. Magical Acoustic Phenomena

sentences.en: Heaven's Heart Stone: The stone slab in the center of the Circular Mound is called "Heaven's Heart Stone." Standing on it and speaking softly, your voice will be particularly loud, like an echo—this is the ancients using the principle of sound reflection and superposition to make the "Son of Heaven's" voice "reach heaven";
sentences.zh: 圜丘坛有两个"声学奇迹"：天心石：圜丘中心的石板叫"天心石"，站在上面轻声说话，声音会特别洪亮，像有回声——这是古人利用声音反射叠加的原理，让"天子"的声音能"传到上天"；回音壁：两个人分别站在回音壁的东西两端，对着墙小声说话，对方能清楚听到，像"天然对讲机"——因为墙面光滑坚硬，声音能沿墙连续反射，不散失。
sentences.heading_en: 3. Magical Acoustic Phenomena

sentences.en: Echo Wall: If two people stand at the east and west ends of the Echo Wall respectively and speak softly to the wall, the other person can hear clearly, like a "natural walkie-talkie"—because the wall is smooth and hard, sound can reflect continuously along the wall without dissipation.

sentences.en: In the past, the emperor's heaven-worship ceremony was very grand, with nine steps:
sentences.zh: 当年皇帝祭天，仪式特别隆重，分九步走：1)迎帝神：把"皇天上帝"的神牌请上祭台；2)奠玉帛：献上玉璧和丝绸；3)进俎：献上牲畜祭品；4)初献：皇帝第一次敬酒，读祭文；5)亚献：皇帝第二次敬酒；6)终献：皇帝第三次敬酒；7)撤馔：撤走祭品；8)送帝神：把神牌送回皇穹宇；9)望燎：把祭文、祭品烧掉，让"上天"收到。

sentences.en: After visiting the Circular Mound Altar, let's walk north along the stone road—this is Danbi Bridge, also known as "Haimen Avenue." It looks flat, but in fact, it's high in the middle and low on both sides:
sentences.zh: 逛完圜丘坛，咱们沿着眼前这条石板路往北走——这就是丹陛桥，也叫"海墁大道"。它看着平，其实中间高、两边低：中间的御道专供皇帝走，左边是王公贵族的路，右边是文武百官的路，等级分得特别清。它的作用就是"过渡"：从南边祭天的"天界"，走到北边祈谷的"人间"，体现"天人相通"。
sentences.heading_en: IV. Danbi Bridge – The "Heavenly Road"

sentences.en: the central imperial road is dedicated to the emperor, the left road is for princes and nobles, and the right road is for civil and military officials—hierarchy is clearly divided.
sentences.zh: 逛完圜丘坛，咱们沿着眼前这条石板路往北走——这就是丹陛桥，也叫"海墁大道"。它看着平，其实中间高、两边低：中间的御道专供皇帝走，左边是王公贵族的路，右边是文武百官的路，等级分得特别清。它的作用就是"过渡"：从南边祭天的"天界"，走到北边祈谷的"人间"，体现"天人相通"。
sentences.heading_en: IV. Danbi Bridge – The "Heavenly Road"

sentences.en: Its role is "transition": from the "heavenly realm" of heaven-worship in the south to the "human world" of harvest-prayer in the north, embodying "the connection between heaven and man."
sentences.zh: 逛完圜丘坛，咱们沿着眼前这条石板路往北走——这就是丹陛桥，也叫"海墁大道"。它看着平，其实中间高、两边低：中间的御道专供皇帝走，左边是王公贵族的路，右边是文武百官的路，等级分得特别清。它的作用就是"过渡"：从南边祭天的"天界"，走到北边祈谷的"人间"，体现"天人相通"。

sentences.en: After walking through Danbi Bridge, we arrive at the Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests, where emperors prayed for "abundant grain harvests" on the first Xin day of the first lunar month every year.
sentences.zh: 走过丹陛桥，咱们就到了祈谷坛，这儿是皇帝每年正月上辛日"祈求五谷丰登"的地方，核心就是咱们眼前这座蓝色圆顶建筑——祈年殿。祈谷坛的组群有三座核心建筑：祈年殿（主殿）：皇帝举行祈谷大典的地方，是天坛的标志性建筑；祈年门（正门）：进入祈谷坛的大门，乾隆皇帝亲笔题的"祈年门"匾额还挂在上面；皇乾殿（北边小殿）：存放祈谷神牌的地方，里面供奉"皇天上帝"和列祖列宗的神牌。
sentences.heading_en: V. Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests – The Emperor's "Sacred Place for Harvest-Prayer" — 1. Architectural Complex & Function

sentences.en: A place to store divine tablets for harvest-prayer, housing the divine tablets of "Heavenly Emperor" and ancestors.
sentences.zh: 走过丹陛桥，咱们就到了祈谷坛，这儿是皇帝每年正月上辛日"祈求五谷丰登"的地方，核心就是咱们眼前这座蓝色圆顶建筑——祈年殿。祈谷坛的组群有三座核心建筑：祈年殿（主殿）：皇帝举行祈谷大典的地方，是天坛的标志性建筑；祈年门（正门）：进入祈谷坛的大门，乾隆皇帝亲笔题的"祈年门"匾额还挂在上面；皇乾殿（北边小殿）：存放祈谷神牌的地方，里面供奉"皇天上帝"和列祖列宗的神牌。
sentences.heading_en: V. Altar of Prayer for Good Harvests – The Emperor's "Sacred Place for Harvest-Prayer" — 1. Architectural Complex & Function

sentences.en: Take a close look at the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests—its "magic" is hidden in the details:
sentences.zh: 大家仔细看祈年殿，它的"神奇"藏在细节里：无钉木构：高38米的三层圆形大殿，全靠木榫卯结构搭建，没用一根钉子；蓝色琉璃瓦：屋顶覆盖蓝色琉璃瓦，因为"蓝色对应天空"，象征与天相通；28根楠木柱：殿内28根楠木柱分三层：最内层4根"龙井柱"代表四季，中间12根"金柱"代表12个月，最外层12根"檐柱"代表12时辰，28根合起来对应28星宿——古人把"天、时、节"全融进建筑里了！
sentences.heading_en: 2. Architectural Features: A Wooden Structure Miracle

sentences.en: In 1998, the Temple of Heaven was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, praised as "the pinnacle of ancient Chinese heaven-worship architecture, embodying the concept of harmonious coexistence between man and nature."
sentences.zh: 1998年，天坛被联合国教科文组织列入《世界遗产名录》，评价它是"中国古代祭天建筑的巅峰之作，体现了人与自然和谐共生的理念"。
sentences.heading_en: VI. World Honor

sentences.en: Dear friends, our tour of the Temple of Heaven ends here!
sentences.zh: 各位朋友，天坛之旅到这儿就结束啦！咱们从圜丘坛的"9数密码"，走到祈年殿的"无钉木构"，听了回音壁的"天然对讲机"，也懂了丹陛桥的"天人之路"。这座600年的圣地，不只是建筑，更是古人"敬天、爱人、祈丰"的初心。希望今天的讲解，能让大家记住天坛的蓝瓦、圆顶，还有那份对自然的敬畏。感谢大家的陪伴，祝大家旅途顺利，再见！
sentences.heading_en: Farewell Speech

sentences.en: This 600-year-old sacred place is not just architecture, but the original intention of the ancients to "respect heaven, love people, and pray for harvests." I hope today's explanation can help you remember the blue tiles and domes of the Temple of Heaven, as well as the respect for nature.
